properties.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 23 at 7:18PM MDT until July 23 at 7:45PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 718 PM MDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Sheridan Lake,
or 22 miles south of Cheyenne Wells, and is nearly stationary.

HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to
roofs, siding, and trees.

Locations impacted include...
Sheridan Lake and Towner.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 7:05PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 704 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 7
miles northwest of John Martin Reservoir, or 24 miles northeast of
North La Junta, moving east at 10 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and half inch hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Las Animas, John Martin Reservoir, Blue Lake, and Fort Lyon.

properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 7:04PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 704 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a line of strong
thunderstorms extending from over Milliken to over Carter Lake, or
extending from 8 miles southwest of Greeley to 15 miles southwest of
Fort Collins, moving southeast at 20 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph and pea size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor damage to outdoor objects is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Southwestern Greeley, northeastern Longmont, Firestone, Johnstown,
Frederick, Milliken, Berthoud, Mead, Platteville, LaSalle,
Gilcrest, Milton Reservoir, Carter Lake, Fort St Vrain, Kelim,
Peckham and Campion.
properties.areaDesc: Larimer County Below 6000 Feet/Northwest Weld County; Boulder And Jefferson Counties Below 6000 Feet/West Broomfield County; Central and South Weld County

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 6:59PM MDT until July 23 at 9:30PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 659 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain over the North and Middle Aspen Acres Burn Scar. Between 0.3 and
0.8 inches of rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 2
inches in 1 hour. Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly. Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in
debris flow. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms producing
flash flooding in and around the North and Middle Aspen
Acres Burn Scar.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around the
North and Middle Aspen Acres Burn Scar.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Beulah.

This includes the following high risk locations...
Beulah Water Intake, Northcreek Road county line to Red Mountain Camp
and Northcreek Road from Red Mountain Camp to Beulah.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
North Red Creek, Middle Red Creek, North Creek and its tributaries,
and North St Charles river east of Beulah.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 23 at 6:41PM MDT until July 23 at 9:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...Southeastern Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 945 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Debris flows causing minor flooding possible over the Alexander
Mountain burn area and portions of Buckhorn Creek within the
Cameron Peak burn area.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 639 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is
ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area.
Between 0.3 and 0.6 inches of rain have fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Sheep Creek, Redstone Creek, Big Thompson River, Buckhorn Creek,
Dry Creek, North Fork Fish Creek and Cedar Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Cedar Cove.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 23 at 6:05PM MDT until July 23 at 9:15PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Arroyo and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of southwest Colorado, including the following
county, San Miguel.

* WHEN...Until 915 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Rises in small streams and normally dry arroyos.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 604 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This will cause arroyo and small stream
flooding. Between 0.3 and 0.8 inches of rain have fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Telluride and Mountain Village including areas along Highway
145 east of Sawpit towards Telluride.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 4:32PM MDT until July 23 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
FFWBOU

The National Weather Service in Denver has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Southeastern Elbert County in east central Colorado...
Central Lincoln County in east central Colorado...

* Until 730 PM MDT.

* At 432 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1 and 2.5 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 1 inch are possible
in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, gulches,
roads, and poor drainage and low-lying areas.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of Southeastern Elbert and Central Lincoln
Counties.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Apache Creek, Mustang Creek, Antelope Creek, South Rush Creek,
Middle Rush Creek, Long Creek, Rush Creek and North Rush Creek.
